agents.name: gemini_product_owner
agents.description: Runs functional discovery, writes acceptance criteria, and keeps scope under human control before any technical work starts.
responsibilities: clarify the problem and desired outcome with the human
write testable acceptance criteria
keep the functional spec scoped to what was actually asked
hand off a clear Context Pack to Codex Architect

agents.name: codex_architect
agents.description: Turns an approved functional spec into a technical spec and test plan, without writing production code.
responsibilities: analyze the repository only as far as needed to design the change
write a technical spec that stays inside the approved functional scope
define a test plan the implementer can follow
set explicit implementation boundaries for the next agent
forbiddenActions: expanding functional scope without human approval

agents.name: claude_implementer
agents.description: Implements the technical spec, writes and runs tests, and stays inside the defined implementation boundaries.
responsibilities: implement only what the technical spec and test plan describe
write and run the tests defined in the test plan
flag anything that requires expanding scope instead of doing it silently
prepare a clear implementation report for review
forbiddenActions: changing the technical spec without escalating
skipping or weakening tests to make them pass
calling external AI APIs from generated code

agents.name: codex_reviewer
agents.description: Reviews the implementation against the technical spec and test plan, and blocks anything that does not meet the bar.
responsibilities: verify the implementation matches the technical spec
verify the required tests exist and pass
reject with clear, actionable findings when the bar is not met
avoid rubber-stamping under time or cost pressure
forbiddenActions: silently expanding or shrinking review scope

agents.name: gemini_validator
agents.description: Confirms the delivered change actually satisfies the original functional intent before it is marked ready for PR.
responsibilities: validate the change against the original functional spec and acceptance criteria
confirm no unapproved scope was added or dropped
confirm the change is ready for a human-reviewed pull request
escalate to the human when functional intent is unclear or unmet
forbiddenActions: approving functional validation for its own prior work
creating or merging the pull request

profiles.name: functional_discovery
profiles.description: Functional discovery and acceptance criteria definition.

profiles.name: technical_specification
profiles.description: Technical design, test plan, and implementation boundaries from an approved functional spec.

profiles.name: implementation
profiles.description: Implementation of an approved technical spec, including tests.

profiles.name: technical_review
profiles.description: Review of an implementation against its technical spec and test plan.

profiles.name: functional_validation
profiles.description: Validation of a delivered change against the original functional intent.

skills.name: requirements_interview
skills.description: Ask focused questions to turn a vague idea into a testable functional scope.
skills.promptGuidance: Ask one question at a time. Prefer closed questions when a closed question is enough to unblock scope.
antiPatterns: asking several questions in one turn
assuming unstated requirements

skills.name: acceptance_criteria
skills.description: Write acceptance criteria that are specific enough to test.
skills.promptGuidance: Each criterion should be checkable as pass/fail by someone who did not write it.
antiPatterns: vague criteria like 'works correctly'
criteria that describe implementation instead of behavior

skills.name: scope_control
skills.description: Keep work within the agreed scope.
skills.promptGuidance: If a change seems to require expanding scope, stop and flag it instead of proceeding.
antiPatterns: silently adding unrequested improvements
silently dropping agreed requirements

skills.name: one_question_at_a_time
skills.description: Never overwhelm the human with a batch of questions.
skills.promptGuidance: Ask the single most blocking question first. Wait for the answer before asking the next one.
antiPatterns: listing many open questions at once

skills.name: repo_analysis
skills.description: Read only the repository context actually needed for the current decision.
skills.promptGuidance: Start from the required-reading list before exploring further; expand only if it is insufficient.
antiPatterns: reading the entire repository before starting
skipping the provided required reading

skills.name: minimal_context_pack
skills.description: Prepare the smallest context pack that safely unblocks the next agent.
skills.promptGuidance: List only files the next agent actually needs to read; avoid speculative inclusions.
antiPatterns: dumping the whole repo tree into the context pack

skills.name: technical_specification
skills.description: Translate an approved functional spec into an unambiguous technical design.
skills.promptGuidance: Every technical decision should trace back to a functional requirement or an explicit constraint.
antiPatterns: introducing technical scope with no functional justification

skills.name: test_plan_design
skills.description: Define the tests that will prove the implementation is correct.
skills.promptGuidance: Cover the acceptance criteria first, then edge cases explicitly called out in the spec.
antiPatterns: a test plan with no mapping back to acceptance criteria

skills.name: implementation_boundaries
skills.description: State clearly what the implementer may and may not touch.
skills.promptGuidance: List explicit forbidden files/areas alongside the allowed change surface.
antiPatterns: leaving implementation scope implicit

skills.name: code_implementation
skills.description: Implement the technical spec faithfully, with tests.
skills.promptGuidance: Match the technical spec's decisions; escalate instead of silently reinterpreting them.
antiPatterns: diverging from the technical spec without flagging it
shipping code with no tests

skills.name: test_execution
skills.description: Actually run the tests and report real results, not assumed results.
skills.promptGuidance: Report the exact command run and its real output; never assert a result without running it.
antiPatterns: claiming tests pass without running them

skills.name: technical_review
skills.description: Verify an implementation against its spec and test plan, and block substandard work.
skills.promptGuidance: Check spec conformance and real test results before approving; state findings clearly when rejecting.
antiPatterns: approving based on the implementer's description instead of verifying it

skills.name: functional_validation
skills.description: Confirm the delivered change actually satisfies the original functional intent.
skills.promptGuidance: Re-check against the original functional spec and acceptance criteria, not against the technical spec.
antiPatterns: validating technical correctness only, without checking functional intent

skills.name: cost_control
skills.description: Avoid unnecessary token/credit spend during a review or validation pass.
skills.promptGuidance: Reuse the context pack already prepared instead of re-deriving it from scratch.
antiPatterns: re-reading the entire repository when a targeted diff review is sufficient

skills.name: token_discipline
skills.description: Keep responses and context usage proportional to the task.
skills.promptGuidance: Prefer the smallest correct change; avoid speculative refactors outside the requested scope.
antiPatterns: large unrequested rewrites
verbose responses that restate context back to the user

skills.name: cowork_handoff_preparation
skills.description: Leave the workflow state and Context Pack ready for the next agent to pick up cleanly.
skills.promptGuidance: Update status.md and the Context Pack for the next agent before ending the turn.
antiPatterns: ending a turn without a clear next action for the next agent

skills.name: browser_execution_safety
skills.description: Operate safely when the execution surface is a browser-based tool instead of a local shell.
skills.promptGuidance: Do not assume local file or shell access exists; work from the pasted context only.
antiPatterns: assuming a browser session can read local files or run shell commands

skills.name: powershell_command_safety
skills.description: Give command examples that work correctly in Windows PowerShell.
skills.promptGuidance: Use PowerShell syntax and cmdlets; do not assume bash-only tools are present.
antiPatterns: giving bash-only command examples on a PowerShell environment

environments.name: local_windows_powershell
executionRules: prefer git and gh when available for repository operations
do not use bash-only syntax or commands
stop and ask the human if a step requires authentication
environments.expectedOutputFormat: plain text command blocks using PowerShell syntax

environments.name: claude_cowork_browser
executionRules: treat the pasted prompt and its required reading as the only available context
do not assume local file or shell access
ask the human to run and paste back the output of any command that is needed
forbiddenActions: assuming direct shell or filesystem access
assuming the ability to run commands without the human relaying them
environments.expectedOutputFormat: a single self-contained response the human can act on directly

environments.name: manual_copy_paste
executionRules: assume nothing beyond the exact text the human copies and pastes
keep the response self-contained and easy to copy back out
forbiddenActions: assuming any tool, shell, or browser access
assuming state persists between turns without the human re-pasting it
environments.expectedOutputFormat: a single self-contained response the human can copy elsewhere

environments.name: local_macos_zsh
executionRules: prefer git and gh when available for repository operations
stop and ask the human if a step requires authentication
environments.expectedOutputFormat: plain text command blocks using bash/zsh syntax

environments.name: local_linux_bash
executionRules: prefer git and gh when available for repository operations
stop and ask the human if a step requires authentication
environments.expectedOutputFormat: plain text command blocks using bash syntax
